raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need grep -R "foo" /system | tee? Actually output to console and count lines. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/stderr? Wait console is stdout. We can pipe to tee? But we want to display matches and then number of matched lines. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/stderr? No. Simpler: grep -R "foo" /system | tee >(wc -l) but that would output count separately. We can use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l? But we need to print matches then number. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l? That would print matches then count. But tee writes to stdout and /dev/tty. Actually tee writes to stdout and file. We can use: grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. But that will output matches then count. However tee will output to stdout, then wc reads from that same stream? Actually pipeline: grep | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. tee writes to stdout and /dev/tty. wc reads from tee's stdout, counting lines. So final output will be matches (from tee to stdout) then count from wc. But wc will output count after matches. Good. Use: grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. But /dev/tty may not be available. Use /dev/stdout? Actually we want to display matches to console; tee to /dev/null? Wait tee duplicates to file. We can just use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/stdout | wc -l? That duplicates to same stream causing double? Actually tee writes to stdout and file. If file is /dev/stdout, it's same as stdout, causing duplicate. Instead we can use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/null | wc -l? That would not show matches. So better: grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. Assume tty. I'll output that.
